news_project
  • news

news package

Submodules

news.admin module

class news.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin view for the Article model.

Provides filtering by approval status, publisher, and creation date. Includes a custom action to batch approve articles.

actions = ['approve_articles']
approve_articles(request, queryset)[source]

Action to approve selected articles.

Updates the is_approved field to True for all selected articles in the queryset.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • queryset (django.db.models.query.QuerySet) – The queryset of selected Article objects.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'is_approved', 'created_at')
list_filter = ('is_approved', 'publisher', 'created_at')
property media
search_fields = ('title', 'content', 'author__username')
class news.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: UserAdmin

Customizes the admin panel view for the CustomUser model.

This admin class extends the default UserAdmin to include specific roles, affiliations, and subscriptions associated with the custom user model.

add_fieldsets = ((None, {'classes': ('wide',), 'fields': ('username', 'usable_password', 'password1', 'password2')}), ('Role & Affiliations', {'fields': ('role', 'publisher_affiliation')}))
fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role & Affiliations', {'fields': ('role', 'publisher_affiliation')}), ('Subscriptions (Readers)', {'description': 'Only applicable if the user has the Reader role.', 'fields': ('subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))
list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'publisher_affiliation', 'is_staff')
list_filter = ('role', 'publisher_affiliation', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'is_active')
property media
search_fields = ('username', 'email')
class news.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin view for the Newsletter model.

Provides filtering by approval status, publisher, and creation date. Includes a custom action to batch approve newsletters.

actions = ['approve_newsletters']
approve_newsletters(request, queryset)[source]

Action to approve selected newsletters.

Updates the is_approved field to True for all selected newsletters in the queryset.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • queryset (django.db.models.query.QuerySet) – The queryset of selected Newsletter objects.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'is_approved', 'created_at')
list_filter = ('is_approved', 'publisher', 'created_at')
property media
search_fields = ('title', 'content', 'author__username')
class news.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin view for the Publisher model.

Provides basic list display and search functionality for Publishers.

list_display = ('name', 'created_at')
property media
search_fields = ('name',)

news.api_urls module

API URL configuration for the news app.

This module defines the URL patterns for the API, using Django REST Framework’s routers to automatically generate URLs for viewsets.

news.api_views module

API views for the news app.

This module defines the viewsets for the API, handling the retrieval of articles and other resources.

class news.api_views.SubscribedArticleViewSet(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ReadOnlyModelViewSet

API endpoint for retrieving subscribed articles.

Allows a third-party client (authenticated user) to retrieve approved articles based on their specific publisher and journalist subscriptions.

basename = None
description = None
detail = None
get_queryset()[source]

Get the queryset of articles for the current user.

Filters articles based on the authenticated user’s subscriptions (publishers and journalists). Only returns approved articles. If the user is not a Reader, returns an empty queryset.

Returns:

A queryset of Article objects.

Return type:

django.db.models.query.QuerySet

name = None
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
serializer_class

alias of ArticleSerializer

suffix = None

news.apps module

App configuration for the news app.

This module configures the ‘news’ application, including default auto field settings and signal registration on startup.

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

Configuration class for the ‘news’ application.

This class sets up the default auto field type and registers signals when the application is ready.

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
name = 'news'
ready()[source]

Perform application initialization tasks.

This method is called when the app is fully loaded. It imports the news.signals module to ensure that signal handlers (receivers) are registered and active.

news.decorators module

Decorators for the news app.

This module provides custom decorators to restrict access to views based on user roles (Editor, Journalist).

news.decorators.editor_required(function=None, redirect_field_name=None, login_url='login')[source]

Decorator for views that checks that the user is logged in and is an editor.

If the user is authenticated but not an editor, a PermissionDenied exception is raised. If not authenticated, redirects to login.

Parameters:

  • function (function) – The view function to decorate.

  • redirect_field_name (str) – The name of the field storing the redirect URL.

  • login_url (str) – The URL to redirect to if not authenticated.

Returns:

The decorated function.

Return type:

function

news.decorators.journalist_required(function=None, redirect_field_name=None, login_url='login')[source]

Decorator for views that checks that the user is logged in and is a journalist.

If the user is authenticated but not a journalist, a PermissionDenied exception is raised. If not authenticated, redirects to login.

Parameters:

  • function (function) – The view function to decorate.

  • redirect_field_name (str) – The name of the field storing the redirect URL.

  • login_url (str) – The URL to redirect to if not authenticated.

Returns:

The decorated function.

Return type:

function

news.forms module

Forms for the news app.

This module defines the forms used for:

  • User registration (RegistrationForm).

  • Article creation and updates (ArticleForm).

  • Newsletter creation and updates (NewsletterForm).

  • Publisher creation (PublisherForm).

class news.forms.ArticleForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for Journalists to draft new articles.

This form allows creating an Article instance with a title and content. The author and publisher are handled in the view.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

Meta options for the ArticleForm.

Specifies the model and fields, and customizes widgets for a better UI.

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']
model

alias of Article

widgets = {'content': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.widgets.Select object>, 'title': <django.forms.widgets.TextInput object>}
base_fields = {'content': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.models.ModelChoiceField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.NewsletterForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for Journalists to draft new newsletters.

This form allows creating a Newsletter instance with a title and content.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

Meta options for the NewsletterForm.

Specifies the model and fields, and customizes widgets for a better UI.

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']
model

alias of Newsletter

widgets = {'content': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.widgets.Select object>, 'title': <django.forms.widgets.TextInput object>}
base_fields = {'content': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.models.ModelChoiceField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.PublisherForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for Editors to add new Publishers to the platform.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

Meta options for the PublisherForm.

Specifies the model and fields, and customizes widgets for a better UI.

fields = ['name', 'description']
model

alias of Publisher

widgets = {'description': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>, 'name': <django.forms.widgets.TextInput object>}
base_fields = {'description': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'name': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.RegistrationForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: UserCreationForm

Form for registering a new user with a specific role.

This form extends the default UserCreationForm to include email and role fields. It also applies Bootstrap classes to form fields for styling.

class Meta[source]

Bases: Meta

Meta options for the RegistrationForm.

Specifies the model (CustomUser) and the fields to be displayed.

fields = ('username', 'email', 'role')
model

alias of CustomUser

base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.TypedChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.contrib.auth.forms.UsernameField object>}
declared_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

news.models module

Database models for the news app.

This module defines the database schema for the application, including custom users, publishers, articles, and newsletters.

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Model representing a news article.

Variables:

  • title (str) – The title of the article.

  • content (str) – The main content of the article.

  • author (CustomUser) – The user who wrote the article.

  • publisher (Publisher) – The publisher associated with the article (optional).

  • is_approved (bool) – Whether the article has been approved by an editor.

  • created_at (datetime) – The timestamp when the article was created.

  • updated_at (datetime) – The timestamp when the article was last updated.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id
content

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_previous_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)

class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model handling Readers, Editors, and Journalists.

This model extends the default Django AbstractUser to include roles and subscriptions.

Variables:

  • email (str) – The unique email address of the user.

  • role (str) – The role of the user (Reader, Editor, or Journalist).

  • subscribed_publishers (django.db.models.query.QuerySet) – Publishers the user is subscribed to (if Reader).

  • subscribed_journalists (django.db.models.query.QuerySet) – Journalists the user is subscribed to (if Reader).

  • publisher_affiliation (Publisher) – The publisher the user works for (if Editor or Journalist).

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

class Role(*values)[source]

Bases: TextChoices

Enumeration for user roles.

Defines the three main user roles: Reader, Editor, and Journalist.

EDITOR = 'editor'
JOURNALIST = 'journalist'
READER = 'reader'
auth_token

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

authored_articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

authored_newsletters

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)
get_role_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: role>)
groups

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

property independent_published_articles

Get articles published independently by a Journalist.

Returns:

A queryset of articles with no publisher or None if not a Journalist.

Return type:

django.db.models.query.QuerySet or None

property independent_published_newsletters

Get newsletters published independently by a Journalist.

Returns:

A queryset of newsletters with no publisher or None if not a Journalist.

Return type:

django.db.models.query.QuerySet or None

publisher_affiliation_id
property reader_journalist_subscriptions

Get the journalist subscriptions for a Reader.

Returns:

A queryset of subscribed journalists or None if not a Reader.

Return type:

django.db.models.query.QuerySet or None

property reader_publisher_subscriptions

Get the publisher subscriptions for a Reader.

Returns:

A queryset of subscribed publishers or None if not a Reader.

Return type:

django.db.models.query.QuerySet or None

reader_subscribers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

role

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Override the save method to enforce role-based field restrictions.

Ensures that Journalists/Editors cannot have Reader subscriptions, and Readers cannot have Publisher affiliations.

Parameters:

  • args – Variable length argument list.

  • kwargs – Arbitrary keyword arguments.

subscribed_journalists

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

subscribed_publishers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

class news.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Model representing a newsletter.

Variables:

  • title (str) – The title/subject of the newsletter.

  • content (str) – The content of the newsletter.

  • author (CustomUser) – The user who wrote the newsletter.

  • publisher (Publisher) – The publisher associated with the newsletter (optional).

  • is_approved (bool) – Whether the newsletter has been approved by an editor.

  • created_at (datetime) – The timestamp when the newsletter was created.

class news.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Model representing a publication/publisher.

Publishers can have multiple editors and journalists assigned to them.

Variables:

  • name (str) – The name of the publisher.

  • description (str) – A description of the publisher.

  • created_at (datetime) – The timestamp when the publisher was created.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

staff

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

news.permissions module

Custom permissions for the news app.

This module defines custom permission classes to control access to API views based on user roles (Reader, Editor, Journalist) and object ownership.

class news.permissions.IsAuthorOrEditor[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Custom object-level permission to only allow the original author of an article/newsletter to edit it, UNLESS the user is an Editor (who has global override rights).

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]

Check object-level permissions.

Allows read-only access for safe methods. Allows write access if the user is an Editor or the author of the object.

Parameters:

  • request (rest_framework.request.Request) – The incoming request object.

  • view (rest_framework.views.APIView) – The view being accessed.

  • obj (news.models.Article or news.models.Newsletter) – The object being accessed.

Returns:

True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

class news.permissions.IsEditor[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allows access only to users with the Editor role.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Check if the user has the ‘Editor’ role.

Parameters:

  • request (rest_framework.request.Request) – The incoming request object.

  • view (rest_framework.views.APIView) – The view being accessed.

Returns:

True if the user is authenticated and is an Editor, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

class news.permissions.IsJournalist[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allows access only to users with the Journalist role.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Check if the user has the ‘Journalist’ role.

Parameters:

  • request (rest_framework.request.Request) – The incoming request object.

  • view (rest_framework.views.APIView) – The view being accessed.

Returns:

True if the user is authenticated and is a Journalist, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

class news.permissions.IsReader[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allows access only to users with the Reader role.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Check if the user has the ‘Reader’ role.

Parameters:

  • request (rest_framework.request.Request) – The incoming request object.

  • view (rest_framework.views.APIView) – The view being accessed.

Returns:

True if the user is authenticated and is a Reader, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

news.serializers module

Serializers for the news app.

This module defines the serializers used to convert model instances into JSON format for the API.

class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializes Article models into JSON format for API consumption.

This serializer handles the conversion of Article objects to JSON, including read-only string representations for related author and publisher fields.

Variables:

  • author (rest_framework.serializers.StringRelatedField) – The string representation of the article’s author.

  • publisher (rest_framework.serializers.StringRelatedField) – The string representation of the article’s publisher.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

Meta options for ArticleSerializer.

Variables:

  • model – The model associated with this serializer.

  • fields – The list of fields to include in the serialization.

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'publisher', 'created_at', 'updated_at']
model

alias of Article

news.signals module

Signals for the news app.

Handles automatic assignment of users to Groups and ensures those Groups have the exact permissions specified in the project outline.

news.signals.assign_user_to_group(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]

Assigns a user to a Django Group based on their role after saving.

This signal receiver listens for the post_save signal of CustomUser. It clears existing groups and adds the user to the group corresponding to their role.

Parameters:

  • sender (type) – The model class.

  • instance (CustomUser) – The actual instance being saved.

  • created (bool) – A boolean; True if a new record was created.

  • kwargs (dict) – Additional keyword arguments.

news.signals.setup_group_permissions(group_name)[source]

Helper function to assign specific model permissions to a group.

Creates the group if it doesn’t exist and assigns default permissions based on the group name (Reader, Editor, Journalist).

Parameters:

group_name (str) – The name of the group (e.g., ‘Reader’, ‘Editor’).

Returns:

The created or retrieved Group object.

Return type:

django.contrib.auth.models.Group

news.tests module

Tests for the news app.

This module contains tests for the API endpoints and other functionalities of the news application, ensuring correct behavior for subscriptions and access controls.

class news.tests.SubscribedArticleAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: APITestCase

Test suite for the third-party RESTful API endpoint that retrieves articles based on a user’s subscriptions.

This test case verifies: - Unauthenticated access is forbidden. - Non-reader roles receive empty lists (as they don’t have subscriptions). - Readers receive only approved articles from their subscriptions.

setUp()[source]

Set up the database state with Publishers, Users, and Articles before each test runs.

Creates: - Two publishers (Tech, Sports) - Users with roles: Journalist, Editor, Reader - Subscriptions for the Reader (Tech Publisher, Alice Journalist) - Four articles with varying approval status and authorship to test filtering logic.

test_non_reader_access()[source]

Ensure Editors or Journalists get an empty list.

This test verifies that users with roles other than READER (e.g., EDITOR) receive an empty list from the endpoint, as the subscription logic is specific to Reader profiles.

test_reader_subscription_results()[source]

Ensure a Reader only receives appropriate articles.

This test verifies that a Reader receives articles that are: 1. From a Publisher they are subscribed to. 2. From a Journalist they are subscribed to. 3. Approved by an Editor.

It also verifies that unapproved articles and articles from sources the user is not subscribed to are excluded.

test_unauthenticated_access()[source]

Ensure unauthenticated users cannot access the API.

This test verifies that a request without authentication credentials returns a 403 Forbidden status code.

news.urls module

URL configuration for the news app.

This module defines the URL patterns for the news application, mapping URLs to their corresponding views. It includes paths for:

  • Public article listing and details.

  • Journalist actions (creating articles).

  • Editor actions (dashboard, approving articles).

  • User authentication (registration, login, logout).

  • Subscription management.

news.views module

Views for the news app.

This module defines the views that handle HTTP requests for the news application. It includes views for: - User registration and subscription management. - Article listing and details (public). - Journalist article creation. - Editor dashboard and article approval.

news.views.approve_article(request, pk)[source]

Approve an article and notify subscribers.

Sets the article’s is_approved status to True. Sends email notifications to subscribers of the publisher and the journalist.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – The primary key of the article to approve.

Returns:

An HTTP response (confirmation page or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.approve_newsletter(request, pk)[source]

Approve a newsletter and notify subscribers via HTML/Text email.

Sets the newsletter’s is_approved status to True. Sends email notifications to all users subscribed to the newsletter’s publisher and/or author.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – The primary key of the newsletter to approve.

Returns:

An HTTP response (confirmation page or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.article_detail(request, pk)[source]

Display the details of a single article.

Retrieves an article by its primary key. The article must be approved.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – The primary key of the article.

Returns:

An HTTP response with the rendered article detail.

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.article_list(request)[source]

Display a list of all approved articles.

Fetches all articles marked as approved, ordered by creation date (newest first).

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response with the rendered article list.

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.create_article(request)[source]

Allow a Journalist to create a new article.

Displays a form for creating an article. Upon valid submission, assigns the current user as the author and their affiliated publisher (if any) to the article. The article is created as unapproved.

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response (rendered form or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.create_newsletter(request)[source]

Allow a Journalist to create a new newsletter.

Displays a form for creating a newsletter. Upon valid submission, assigns the current user as the author. The newsletter is created as unapproved by default.

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response (rendered form or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.create_publisher(request)[source]

Allow an Editor to create a new Publisher entity.

Presents a form for an editor to add a new publisher to the platform.

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response (rendered form or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.delete_article(request, pk)[source]

Allow a Journalist to delete their own article.

Presents a confirmation page before deleting an article. Only the original author can perform this action.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – The primary key of the article to delete.

Returns:

An HTTP response (confirmation page or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

Raises:

PermissionDenied – If the user is not the author of the article.

news.views.delete_newsletter(request, pk)[source]

Allow a Journalist to delete their own newsletter.

Presents a confirmation page before deleting a newsletter. Only the original author can perform this action.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – The primary key of the newsletter to delete.

Returns:

An HTTP response (confirmation page or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

Raises:

PermissionDenied – If the user is not the author of the newsletter.

news.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]

Display a dashboard for a journalist.

Shows lists of articles and newsletters authored by the currently logged-in journalist, allowing them to manage their content.

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response with the rendered journalist dashboard.

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.newsletter_detail(request, pk)[source]

Display the details of a single, approved newsletter.

Retrieves a newsletter by its primary key. The newsletter must be approved to be viewed by the public.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – The primary key of the newsletter.

Returns:

An HTTP response with the rendered newsletter detail.

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.newsletter_list(request)[source]

Display a list of all approved newsletters.

Fetches all newsletters marked as approved, ordered by creation date (newest first).

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response with the rendered newsletter list.

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.publisher_list(request)[source]

Display a list of all publishers.

This view retrieves all Publisher objects from the database, ordered by name, and renders them in a list for users to browse and subscribe.

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response with the rendered publisher list.

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.register(request)[source]

Handle user registration.

Processes the registration form. If valid, saves the new user, logs them in, and redirects to the article list.

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response (rendered template or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.toggle_subscription(request, target_type, target_id)[source]

Toggle a user’s subscription to a publisher or journalist.

Allows a Reader to subscribe or unsubscribe from a specific publisher or journalist. Handles validation of roles.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • target_type (str) – The type of entity to subscribe to (‘publisher’ or ‘journalist’).

  • target_id (int) – The primary key of the target entity.

Returns:

An HTTP redirect response.

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponseRedirect

news.views.unapproved_articles(request)[source]

Display a dashboard for Editors to manage unapproved content.

Fetches all unapproved articles and newsletters. If the editor is affiliated with a publisher, it only shows content for that publisher. Otherwise, it shows all unapproved content on the platform.

Parameters:

request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

An HTTP response with the rendered editor dashboard.

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

news.views.update_article(request, pk)[source]

Allow a Journalist to update their own article.

Fetches an existing article and presents a form to edit it. Only the original author can update their article. Upon submission, the article’s approval status is reset to False, requiring re-approval.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – The primary key of the article to update.

Returns:

An HTTP response (rendered form or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

Raises:

PermissionDenied – If the user is not the author of the article.

news.views.update_newsletter(request, pk)[source]

Allow a Journalist to update their own newsletter.

Fetches an existing newsletter and presents a form to edit it. Only the original author can perform this action. Upon submission, the newsletter’s approval status is reset to False.

Parameters:

  • request (django.http.HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – The primary key of the newsletter to update.

Returns:

An HTTP response (rendered form or redirect).

Return type:

django.http.HttpResponse

Raises:

PermissionDenied – If the user is not the author of the newsletter.

Module contents
